steps.title: Use Vieta's formulas
steps.content:
For a quadratic $$x^2 + bx + c = 0$$, Vieta’s formulas give the sum of the roots $$=-\frac{b}{a}$$ and the product $$=\frac{c}{a}$$ (where the leading coefficient is 1 here).
In $$x^2 - a x + 12 = 0$$ we have $$b = -a$$ and $$c = 12$$.
Therefore
$$\text{sum of roots}= -\frac{-a}{1}=a$$
$$\text{product of roots}= \frac{12}{1}=12$$

steps.title: Find integer factor pairs of 12
steps.content:
Since the roots are integers and their product is 12, we look for integer factor pairs of 12.
Both roots must be either both positive or both negative to give a positive product.
Because the sum equals $$a>0$$, the roots cannot both be negative (that would give a negative sum).
Thus we only consider positive pairs:
  • $$1\times12=12$$ → sum $$1+12=13$$
  • $$2\times6=12$$ → sum $$2+6=8$$
  • $$3\times4=12$$ → sum $$3+4=7$$
Hence the possible values of $$a$$ (the sum) are 13, 8, and 7.

steps.title: Select a possible value
steps.content:
Choosing one of these, take $$a=8$$ (it satisfies all conditions).
The answer is 8.

Equivalent forms accepted: 7, 8, or 13.
